semantics: A projected source provider is an opaque intrusive reference whose backing allocation is independent of the originating VM heap.
Exception source-line BunStrings are non-owning static views into the retained provider rather than independent string allocations.
The provider copies the exact registered debug-script source so views survive realm retirement, precise-GC relocation, and terminal VM teardown.
Collecting source lines replaces and releases any provider already stored in the output trace before publishing a new reference.
Every successfully published provider is released exactly once by ZigException consumer deinitialization or by a later replacement.
The atomic intrusive count permits final release from a consumer thread different from the projection thread.
Null dereference is inert; foreign, stale, and repeated non-null pointers retain the pinned upstream raw-pointer precondition and are caller undefined behavior.
Source offsets and line numbers remain byte-accurate for ASCII, Latin-1, and WTF-8 source while borrowed views carry the matching ZigString encoding tag.
Same-VM sibling realms may collect an exception's source lines, while a foreign VM cannot project or retain the provider.
The symbol belongs only to the Home profile at the pinned revisions and does not change Bun or public C-API accounting.
